goal: Add a contract-only merge/release readiness preflight that plans PR order and receipt readiness before real merges or deploys, without creating PRs, merging, deploying, or calling GitHub.
scope_out: Do not create pull requests, call GitHub, merge branches, deploy, write files, write DB, call providers, execute runtime, issue permits, or change package/workflow files.
claims: Bluepilot can plan a PR sequence from branch candidates without creating PRs.
Bluepilot can evaluate PR receipt readiness without merging.
Application PR creation, merge execution, and deploy execution remain false even when the operator sequence is ready.
assumptions: Actual PR creation, merge, deploy, and receipt recording require later explicit locks or manual operator action.
This surface consumes caller-provided branch and PR metadata only; it does not fetch GitHub.
dod: GET /probe/merge-release-readiness-contract returns the closed release boundary.
POST /probe/merge-release-readiness-preflight validates candidate order and optional PR receipts without GitHub writes.
The eight-point readiness and operator dashboard mark Merge and Release Readiness as wired_contract_only.
Focused tests, typecheck, diff-check, and full builder tests pass.
stop_conditions: A PR creation, GitHub call, merge, deploy, DB write, file write, provider call, runtime execution, permit issue, package change, or workflow change becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: Provider/Runtime Flows are now wired_contract_only; Merge/Release Readiness remains the final eight-point activation surface to wire.
